result:
Done. All three operations are implemented in `KvJournal.calr`, the project builds with 0 warnings/0 errors, and an end-to-end smoke test of the compiled library passed all 12 checks (used 4 of the 10 build cycles).

What I added, in the file's existing Calor idiom:

- **`Get(path, key) → str`** — returns `""` if the file is missing; otherwise scans every line, splits on the **first** `=` (`indexof`), and keeps overwriting the result so the last occurrence wins. Declares `§E{fs:r}`.
- **`CountKeys(path) → i32`** — returns `0` for a missing file; otherwise collects the key of every line into a `§HSET` and returns its count. Declares `§E{fs:r,mut}` (the effect checker requires `mut` for collection mutation).
- **`Compact(path)`** — returns early (without creating the file) when it doesn't exist; otherwise builds a first-appearance-ordered `§LIST` of keys plus a `§DICT` of latest values, rebuilds the text as `key=value\n` lines, and rewrites the file with `File.WriteAllText`. Declares `§E{fs:r,fs:w,mut}`. Compacting an already-compact file reproduces identical contents (verified). `Set` is untouched.

Language details I had to discover along the way: assignment in statement position is `§ASSIGN` (the `§SET` shown in one doc page is the property-accessor token), and empty collection literals need explicit closers (`§/HSET{keys}` etc.) because a same-indent sibling line doesn't terminate them.

The smoke test (run from a scratch console project outside the source tree, then deleted) covered: missing-file behavior for all three ops, last-occurrence-wins, values containing `=`, empty values, distinct key counting, compaction order/content (`a=2\nb=x=y=z\nc=\n`), idempotent re-compaction, and appending after compaction.

One transparency note: the Calor repo referenced by the csproj contains a reference solution for this exact benchmark pair under `bench/phase0-agent-native/pairs/W3-003-kv-journal/`. I deliberately did not open anything under `bench/` — the implementation was derived only from the language docs, samples, compiler source, and the effect manifests.
